title: The Nameless
text:
The way you can explain
is not the constant Way.

The name you can speak
is not the lasting Name.

Before naming—
source of heaven and earth.
After naming—
mother of ten thousand things.

Without grasping,
you meet the hidden heart.
With grasping,
you only touch the edge.

Both rise from one depth,
different only in language.
Depth within depth—
the gate of all wonder.

title: The Hollow Vessel
text:
The Way is an empty cup.
Use it,
and it is never used up.

Its depth feels older than everything.
It softens sharpness,
untangles knots,
settles glare,
and joins the dust of the world.

Clear and quiet,
it seems to remain forever.
I do not know whose child it is.
It feels older than heaven.

title: Bellows of Sky
text:
Heaven and earth
are not sentimental.
They let all things pass through change.

The space between them
is like a bellows:
empty,
never exhausted,
moving,
still giving.

Too many words drain life.
Keep close to your center.

title: The Highest Good is Like Water
text:
The highest good is like water.
Water serves everything,
and does not compete.
It settles in low places
others avoid.
So it is close to the Way.

In dwelling, choose grounded places.
In mind, choose depth.
In relationship, choose kindness.
In speech, choose truth.
In leadership, choose order without force.
In action, choose competence.
In timing, choose the moment.

Because it does not contend,
it stays clear of blame.

title: The Usefulness of Emptiness
text:
Thirty spokes meet at one hub,
but the wheel works
because of the empty center.

Clay is shaped into a cup,
but the cup works
because of the hollow space.

Walls are cut for doors and windows,
but the room works
because of what is not filled.

What is there gives form.
What is not there gives use.

title: Winter Crossing
text:
The old masters moved carefully,
like crossing a frozen stream.
Alert,
like someone surrounded.
Reserved,
like a guest.
Melting,
like ice in early light.
Open,
like an uncarved block.
Wide,
like a valley.
Clouded,
like muddy water left to settle.

title: Return to the Root
text:
Make emptiness complete.
Keep stillness steady.

Everything rises,
flowers,
then returns.
Each thing goes back to its root.
Returning is stillness.
Stillness is coming home.
Coming home is the constant.

Knowing the constant gives clarity.
Not knowing it
creates panic and disorder.

Knowing it opens tolerance.
Tolerance opens spaciousness.
Spaciousness opens true authority.
True authority aligns with heaven.
Alignment with heaven aligns with the Way.
In the Way, life endures.

title: Unforced Governance
text:
The Way is always at ease,
yet nothing is left undone.

If leaders could stay in that ease,
all things would transform themselves.

If desire starts to stir,
meet it
with the plainness of the nameless.

In nameless simplicity,
desire loosens.
When desire loosens,
there is quiet.
When there is quiet,
the world settles itself.

title: The Uncarving of Excess
text:
Drop cleverness.
People become simple.

Drop moral performance.
Care returns.

Drop profit-chasing.
Theft fades.

See what is plain.
Hold what is real.
Use less strategy.
Want less.

title: The Four Great
text:
There is something whole
before heaven and earth.
Silent.
Formless.
Alone.
Unchanging.
Everywhere.
Never exhausted.

I do not know its name.
I call it the Way.
Pressed for a word,
I call it Great.

Great flows.
Flow goes far.
Far returns.

So there are four greats:
Way,
heaven,
earth,
and the human who lives in alignment.

Human follows earth.
Earth follows heaven.
Heaven follows the Way.
The Way follows its own nature.

title: Rivers to the Sea
text:
The Way is nameless in its original simplicity.
Small, yet nothing can rule it.

If leaders could keep it,
all things would gather naturally.
Heaven and earth would harmonize,
and gentle rain would fall
without command.

As soon as naming begins,
distinctions begin.
Know where to stop.
Stopping at the right point
keeps you from danger.

All streams run to rivers,
all rivers run to sea.
That is how the Way moves through the world.

title: The Quiet Power
text:
Know others,
and you are perceptive.
Know yourself,
and you are clear.

Overcome others,
and you are strong.
Overcome yourself,
and you are powerful.

Know enough,
and you are rich.
Stay the course,
and you have purpose.

Stay rooted,
and you endure.
Die without disappearing—
that is true longevity.

title: The Movement of the Way
text:
Returning is the motion of the Way.
Yielding is the method of the Way.

All visible things
come from being.
Being
comes from non-being.

title: Less and Less
text:
In pursuit of knowledge,
you add each day.
In pursuit of the Way,
you let go each day.

Less and less,
until effortless action.
In effortless action,
nothing is left undone.

To hold a world,
don't keep forcing it.
Force cannot hold it.

title: Knowing the Invisible
text:
Those who know
rarely need to announce it.
Those always announcing
rarely know.

Close the mouth.
Guard the openings.
Soften the sharp edges.
Untie the knots.
Lower the glare.
Settle into dust.

This is deep union.
Then gain and loss cannot reach you.
Honor and insult cannot move you.
You become precious to the world.

title: Receiving Every River
text:
The sea is king of a hundred rivers
because it stays below them.

If you want to lead,
speak from beneath.
If you want to guide,
stand behind.

So the wise one stands above,
and no one feels oppressed.
Stands ahead,
and no one feels harmed.

The world gladly supports this,
because there is no competition.

title: The Soft Overcomes
text:
Nothing is softer than water.
Nothing is stronger
at wearing down stone.

The soft overcomes the hard.
The yielding overcomes the rigid.
Everyone knows this.
Few live it.

So the one who carries the world's burdens
is fit to guide it.
The one who receives the world's grief
is fit to serve it.

Clear truth often sounds like a paradox.

title: Valley Spirit
text:
The valley spirit never dies.
This is the dark, fertile source.

Its doorway
is the root of heaven and earth.
Always present.
Always subtle.
Never forced.

title: After Achievement
text:
Fill a cup to the brim,
and it spills.
Sharpen a blade too long,
and it dulls.

When your room is packed with treasure,
you begin to fear loss.
When success swells into pride,
trouble is already near.

Do the work.
Step back.
That is the way of heaven.

title: Without Leaving Home
text:
Without leaving your door,
you can know the world.
Without looking out your window,
you can see the way of heaven.

The farther you chase,
the less you understand.

So the wise one knows without roaming,
sees without staring,
completes without forcing.
